# destroy()

**Instance Method**

Removes underlying allocations and connections from the USB host object.

## Declaration

```swift
func destroy()
```

## Discussion

When you no longer need the [doc://com.apple.iousbhost/documentation/IOUSBHost/IOUSBHostObject], call [doc://com.apple.iousbhost/documentation/IOUSBHost/IOUSBHostObject/destroy()]. This method destroys the connection with the kernel object and deregisters interest on [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/io_service_t]. Calling [doc://com.apple.iousbhost/documentation/IOUSBHost/IOUSBHostObject/destroy()] multiple times has no effect.

## Managing the Object Life Cycle

- **IOUSBHostObjectInitOptions**: Options for initializing the host object.
- **IOUSBHostInterestHandler**: The callback that handles underlying service-state changes.
- **ioService**: A reference to the kernel object.
- **queue**: The queue for servicing input/output requests.
